name: Cancel OCO Order
summary: Cancels an existing OCO (One-Cancels-Other) order/alert via a POST call. The request identifies the OCO order by its alert id (al_id), and the response indicates success or failure.

request_params.name: jData
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user.

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: OCO order success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: This will be present only in case of errors. That is : 1) Invalid Input 2) Session Expired

notes: The curl example uses BaseURL as a placeholder; the actual host is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI (full endpoint .../CancelOCOOrder). The body is sent as form data of the shape jData={...}&jKey=.... In the request JSON, only al_id is mandatory (trailing '*'); uid is optional. On success, stat returns a free-text value (e.g. "Oi delete success") rather than the usual "Ok", and request_time + al_id are echoed back; on failure stat is "Not_Ok" with an emsg describing the error (Invalid Input or Session Expired). The 'Possible value' column is blank for every field in this section. The entire section is contained on page 40 (page 41 begins the Holdings section).
issues: None. The section is fully self-contained on page 40 (title 'CANCEL OCO ORDER' through the right-column samples, ending before 'HOLDINGS AND LIMITS'). The page image confirms the 'Possible value' column is blank for every field, so there are no enum cells to misbind. al_id and both params (jData, jKey) carry the trailing '*' (mandatory); uid does not.
